title: Polemonium brandegeei
taxonomy: Polemonium brandegeei is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Polemoniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the skyrocket or Jacob's ladder. Within the family, Polemonium brandegeei is also part of the genus Polemonium, which includes 18 species of flowering plants native to North America. The genus Polemonium is further classified within the order Ericales. This classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Polemonium brandegeei, also known as Brandegee's Jacob's-ladder, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Polemoniaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, ladder-like spikes of blue to purple flowers. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and reduce oxidative stress.
